author: PC Gamer
text:
Hide-and-seek game where you paint your body to blend in sells a million copies in four days
Meccha Chameleon is the latest indie smash hit centering silly multiplayer antics.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Blizzard sues to kill another World of Warcraft private server for 'large scale, egregious, and ongoing infringement of [its] intellectual property'
Project Ascension is in the MMO studio's sights.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I went to the UK's largest rock festival to see its one and only videogame band
The Primals took to the stage at Download Festival in their first non-Fanfest appearance outside of Asia.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'I try to play as a completionist and leave no stone unturned and no enemy unkilled': Returnal director Harry Krueger talks Deus Ex, Resident Evil, and the arcade classic that inspired Housemarque's best games
The Housemarque veteran guides us through the games on his PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
FromSoftware boss Hidetaka Miyazaki references 'yet to be announced' games while commenting on controversy at parent company Kadokawa
A crumb of FromSoft, exactly like I asked for.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Spore devs say the evolution game's previews were more ambitious than what they were actually making, and they 'built a fantasy in people's minds that was unachievable'
The evolution sim that was never meant to be.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ultima Online and Star Wars Galaxies vet tells me the theme park 'assembly line' MMO just isn't viable anymore, especially as dev costs spike: 'We hit the wall'
"The MMO audience feels underserved and overmonetized, that's how they feel about what's going on."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cave Story+ review (2012)
Underground hit about underground hitting gets an HD upgrade.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mina the Hollower has a secret ending, but good luck seeing it
If you want to see Mina's alternate conclusion, prepare yourself for an ordeal.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Frontier is making a Planet game that isn't about zoos or rollercoasters, and has just filed trademarks for Planet Resort and Planet Festival
Planet crafters.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After gaming on pro-level panels I can tell you now that 360 Hz is the sweet spot for gaming monitors
And I just don't think I can go back to anything below 240 Hz.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This hand-embroidered medieval adventure game looks like a lovely, chill time—but I'm warning you, don't be fooled by its pleasant exterior
Scarlet Deer Inn releases next month.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sony pulling back from PC also means it's pulling back from China
Thanks to restrictive import regulations, console gaming never took off in China the way PC and mobile have.

author: PC Gamer
text:
You can download Planescape: Torment's unofficial DLC mod right now
Cania believe it?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Alongside its new expansion, Warhammer 40K: Rogue Trader got a massive patch buffing its weakest classes and companion
Uralon the Cool.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Nintendo's legal battle with Palworld looks shakier than ever: It may only get '$30,000 chump change' if it wins at all
It looks like Pocketpair will come out of this largely unfazed.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Alright, who's the joker trying to charge $100 on Steam for an almost entirely AI-generated game?
C'mon man.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Make some noise': Vanillaware reportedly wants more of its games on PC, but it's up to the publishers
The studio behind Odin Sphere and Unicorn Overlord "totally wants" more of its games on PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A little known rendering technique that can create low-cost, photo-real graphics may be about to have its big moment in game development
Sheer art attack.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New Crimson Desert patch lets you hoard 100 pets and gives you more chainsaw access
More pets, more chainsaws. What else are videogames for?
